ref: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
text: «Wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ wɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ wɔɔ kɩ, Moyiisi lɛrɔ wɔɔ ŋn'a hɩnkam gʊɔɔ m.
translated: The farmers are in the fields, but Moyiisi is at home with his wife.

ref: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Kʊ m awɔɔ lar mim deem ma, deem bala, k'awɔɔ nyɩ bɔ ʊ, kʊ m bɔkarɛ, m lɛ kʊ mɔɔ n ta m bɩ jɩ hɩ awɔɔ m.
translated: God said to the man, "You will cultivate the land and eat, eat and be full, and your livestock will be many, and you will be rich, and I will be with you and bless you."

ref: They sat down in groups of
text: Ŋ nɩ nyɩnyɩnta zɔrɔ-zɔrɔ, busoor-busoor.
translated: I am very, very tired, completely exhausted.

ref: An ax is ready to cut the trees down at their roots. Any tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into a fire.
text: Bɩ dɩɩtaa naa, n sɩga banka k'an gɔrɔ zɩnzɛ ra ŋ məsi ma, gɔ niŋŋə k'a bɩr nyɩ mɩŋŋa darɛ y, n y'a zɛm la, n n a da sɛ ʊ.» Hi batɛm kan sɛ batɛm kɩ
translated: They said that, I went to the bank to withdraw some money to buy food, but when I got there, I saw a long queue, so I returned, and I didn't get anything.

ref: The seeds that fell on good ground are the people who hear and understand the message. They produce as much as
text: Hɔyaa k'a nɩ lɛɛ mɩŋŋa ʊ bɩ, gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ Woso lemim bɩ ma, ŋn'a jɩ ma rɔ wɔɔ m: Ŋ n'ɩ nyɩ dam. A yaa deem ɩ nyɩ dam zɔrɔ, deem busɔrdɩ, deem bukərku.»
translated: The rabbit said to his sister, "Go and tell Woso that I am dead, and when she comes, say to her: I am not dead. She will go to my dead body, go to my grave, go to my tomb."

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Hɩrɔɔdɩ k'a b'a dɔ a ʊ, hɔdonno wɔɔ a yamba a m bɩ, a wuti nɔmaa gandaara m. A n gʊɔɔ nyɔɔ Betelehɛm ʊ kan kʊrɔ vantɔɔ k'a a so ŋ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ kɩ ʊ, kʊ ŋ n'aa nyɩnɔ poorero yaarɔ kʊ ŋ dɔɔraa hɩɩya gɛɛ ŋ n a ku rɔ wɔɔ zɩnzɛ. Amba hɔdonno wɔɔ a hɩ m, nyɩnta k'a ʊ, an laaka da k'a nyɩ bɩ yɩrɛ dɔmɩm dɔ bɩ ma.
translated: Herod heard that Jesus was born, he was very angry and called all the wise men in the country. He asked them where Jesus was born, they told him Jesus was born in Bethlehem, he then sent his soldiers to go and kill all the children in Bethlehem. But when the angel appeared to Joseph, he took Jesus and went to hide in Egypt until the death of Herod.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: K'a dɩga bɛnnɔ ma: ŋ bɩr hɔ duure y, bɩ ŋ bɩr hɔ darɛ y, ŋ bɩr hɔ zarm sɩ ʊ y, b'awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ yɩ ŋ tʊrm! Awɔɔ n lɛ da ŋ nɩ gallɛm hɔka ra?
translated: They say the ancestors said: don't go to the bush, don't go to the forest, don't go to the riverside, it's where the Zɩ people's ancestors and their fetishes are! Why do you go to the bush to look for trouble?

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”

ref: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
text: Lannɔ nyɩnta b'ʊ guta, ŋn'a to laatʊ ŋn'ɩ dɩgam, zu ŋ y'a ʊ Yeezuu jɛ ʊ Galilee ʊ tee…, ŋn'a dam han.
translated: The ruler of the synagogue was angry, and taking it badly that Jesus healed on the Sabbath in Galilee again..., he was very annoyed.

ref: As soon as she touched them, her bleeding stopped, and she knew she was healed.
text: Lɛɛm bɩ, lʊ bɩ yaaba b'a da jɩm ʊ. A n a dɔ a ʊ, yaaba bɩ bɔ a mɛ ʊ, b'a a waa.
translated: The man is coming, he is the chief's son, don't go near him. I am going to hide from him, the chief's son is looking for me to kill me, they are coming.

ref: When the men went into the house and saw the child with Mary, his mother, they knelt down and worshiped him. They took out their gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh and gave them to him.
text: Ŋ gasʊ cɛ b'ʊ, ŋn'aa nyɩ bɩ yɩ kan a da Maarii kɩ, ŋnɩ kukurə zɛ a taa, ŋnɩ yaa da m, bɩ jɛ ʊ, ŋn'a mɔdrɔ wɔɔ hʊr, ŋnɩ gan wʊ ma: Sarma m kan tidəər nyisi kɩ, *mɩɩr kɩ.
translated: The woman went to the bush, she saw a big animal in the bush at Maarii's place, she saw a tortoise in the bush, she saw that it was big, it was fat, its shell was very hard, she said to her friend: The animal in the bush is very big, it is a rhinoceros.

ref: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.

translated: They said, if you go to the river and your friend is drowning, you, what do you do and your friend dies, your friend's death is your fault.

translated: He went to the lake shore, to the boats of Zebedee's sons, James and John his brother, who were mending their nets in the boat.

translated: And when he drew near and saw the city, Jesus wept over it, saying: "If you knew, even you, especially in this your day, the things that make for your peace, but now they are hidden from your eyes. ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Hɩrɔɔdɩ k'a b'a dɔ a ʊ, hɔdonno wɔɔ a yamba a m bɩ, a wuti nɔmaa gandaara m. A n gʊɔɔ nyɔɔ Betelehɛm ʊ kan kʊrɔ vantɔɔ k'a a so ŋ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ kɩ ʊ, kʊ ŋ n'aa nyɩnɔ poorero yaarɔ kʊ ŋ dɔɔraa hɩɩya gɛɛ ŋ n a ku rɔ wɔɔ zɩnzɛ. Amba hɔdonno wɔɔ a hɩ m, nyɩnta k'a ʊ, an laaka da k'a nyɩ bɩ yɩrɛ dɔmɩm dɔ bɩ ma.
translated: Herod heard that Jesus was born, he was very angry and called all the wise men in the country. He asked them where Jesus was born, they told him Jesus was born in Bethlehem, he then sent his soldiers to go and kill all the children in Bethlehem. But when the angel appeared to Joseph, he took Jesus and went to hide in Egypt until the death of Herod.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: K'a dɩga bɛnnɔ ma: ŋ bɩr hɔ duure y, bɩ ŋ bɩr hɔ darɛ y, ŋ bɩr hɔ zarm sɩ ʊ y, b'awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ yɩ ŋ tʊrm! Awɔɔ n lɛ da ŋ nɩ gallɛm hɔka ra?
translated: They say the ancestors said: don't go to the bush, don't go to the forest, don't go to the riverside, it's where the Zɩ people's ancestors and their fetishes are! Why do you go to the bush to look for trouble?

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Naa kur bɩ, wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ kɩ a sɩŋŋɩda ba, ŋn'a hɩ Yeezuu m, ŋ ʊ: «Karɩnsaamba, wɔɔ lɛ taa k'ɩ hoser ba k'ʊ a yɩ.»
translated: The king said, "When you return, greet the official for me and tell him, 'Sir, I say to you, come down before I have to come and get you.'"

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.

Amba hɔdonno wɔɔ a hɩ m, nyɩnta k'a ʊ, an laaka da k'a nyɩ bɩ yɩrɛ dɔmɩm dɔ bɩ ma.
translated: Herod heard that Jesus was born, he was very angry and called all the wise men in the country. He asked them where Jesus was born, they told him Jesus was born in Bethlehem, he then sent his soldiers to go and kill all the children in Bethlehem. But when the angel appeared to Joseph, he took Jesus and went to hide in Egypt until the death of Herod.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: K'a dɩga bɛnnɔ ma: ŋ bɩr hɔ duure y, bɩ ŋ bɩr hɔ darɛ y, ŋ bɩr hɔ zarm sɩ ʊ y, b'awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ yɩ ŋ tʊrm! Awɔɔ n lɛ da ŋ nɩ gallɛm hɔka ra?
translated: They say the ancestors said: don't go to the bush, don't go to the forest, don't go to the riverside, it's where the Zɩ people's ancestors and their fetishes are! Why do you go to the bush to look for trouble?

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Naa kur bɩ, wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ kɩ a sɩŋŋɩda ba, ŋn'a hɩ Yeezuu m, ŋ ʊ: «Karɩnsaamba, wɔɔ lɛ taa k'ɩ hoser ba k'ʊ a yɩ.»
translated: The king said, "When you return, greet the official for me and tell him, 'Sir, the king says to come to him.'"

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
text: Zibəənyɩ bɩ do nyɩntam mimbirenyɩntazaa, k'a lɛɛzaa b'a wusigə an bʊr har ʊ, an b'a ku an nɩ zibəə k'a ka ʊ bɩ barɛ.
translated: The rabbit and the elephant became friends, and they agreed that they would go to the bush together, and they would share whatever they found, and the rabbit would carry it on his back.

ref: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
text: Bɩ, a muno wɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «Jɩm cɩna, k'ʊ dɩga a Elii zɛm b'a bʊmbɔ gɛ?»
translated: No, my child, what's wrong, he said: "What's going on, is it that you've gone to Eli's field to get something to eat?"

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.

text: K'a dɩga bɛnnɔ ma: ŋ bɩr hɔ duure y, bɩ ŋ bɩr hɔ darɛ y, ŋ bɩr hɔ zarm sɩ ʊ y, b'awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ yɩ ŋ tʊrm! Awɔɔ n lɛ da ŋ nɩ gallɛm hɔka ra?
translated: They say the ancestors said: don't go to the bush, don't go to the forest, don't go to the riverside, it's where the Zɩ people's ancestors and their fetishes are! Why do you go to the bush to look for trouble?

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Naa kur bɩ, wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ kɩ a sɩŋŋɩda ba, ŋn'a hɩ Yeezuu m, ŋ ʊ: «Karɩnsaamba, wɔɔ lɛ taa k'ɩ hoser ba k'ʊ a yɩ.»
translated: The king said, "When you return, greet the official for me and tell him, 'Sir, I say to you, come down before I have to come and get you.'"

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
text: Zibəənyɩ bɩ do nyɩntam mimbirenyɩntazaa, k'a lɛɛzaa b'a wusigə an bʊr har ʊ, an b'a ku an nɩ zibəə k'a ka ʊ bɩ barɛ.
translated: The rabbit and the elephant became friends, and they agreed that they would go to the bush together, and they would share whatever they found, and the rabbit would carry it on his back.

ref: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
text: Bɩ, a muno wɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «Jɩm cɩna, k'ʊ dɩga a Elii zɛm b'a bʊmbɔ gɛ?»
translated: No, my child, what's wrong, he said: "What's going on, is it that you've gone to Eli's field to get something to eat?"
